decision_notes: Official documentation can constrain supported API claims but still needs a local port validation path.

findings.title: Custom kernels need explicit MLX API and fallback validation
findings.summary: Treat custom Metal kernels as supported only when the MLX custom-kernel API path, shape contract, fallback, and parity tests are recorded.
findings.validation_gate: Pin MLX version, test fallback, and compare kernel outputs to a readable MLX or NumPy oracle.
caveats: Official API support does not prove a model-level speedup.
findings.required_next_validation: Run targeted kernel parity and end-to-end benchmarks before promoting any specific kernel.

decision_notes: Model-card popularity helps prioritize architecture coverage but is not implementation evidence.

findings.title: Use Hugging Face task/library metadata to prioritize porting coverage
findings.summary: Hugging Face model metadata can reveal high-demand tasks and artifact shapes, but should feed a coverage backlog rather than supported implementation claims.
findings.validation_gate: Sample model cards, licenses, configs, processors, and source frameworks before adding a runbook or architecture family.
caveats: Metadata does not prove an MLX implementation path.
findings.required_next_validation: Collect representative model cards for each gap and map them to source-oracle requirements.

decision_notes: Practitioner reports are good at revealing failure modes, but claims need independent reproduction.

findings.title: Keep benchmark claims attached to hardware and workload
findings.summary: Blog benchmarks should be ingested as leads unless they include hardware, software version, workload, baseline, and quality gate.
findings.validation_gate: Reproduce benchmark or convert claim into a profile checklist before changing optimization guidance.
caveats: The fixture source is broad and not a specific benchmark claim.
findings.required_next_validation: Identify specific MLX-relevant benchmark posts and record exact protocol details.

limitations: Fixture is deterministic and does not represent a full live research pass.
